name: Fail-Open Sandbox Degradation
summary: Code execution sandboxes silently degrade to insecure fallback modes when the underlying isolation mechanism (Docker, container runtime) becomes unavailable. No user notification, consent, or logging occurs during degradation.
mechanism: When Docker is unavailable at startup or becomes unavailable during runtime, the framework falls back to a Python-based sandbox (SandboxPython) that can be escaped via ctypes to call arbitrary C functions. The fallback is silent — no alert, no consent prompt, no log entry.
blast_radius: Full host-level arbitrary code execution. In multi-agent deployments, one agent can exhaust Docker resources to force other agents into the insecure fallback.
prerequisite: Agent with code execution capability (allow_code_execution=True or CodeInterpreter tool). Docker unavailable or stoppable.

defences.name: Fail-Closed Architecture
defences.description: Refuse code execution if sandbox integrity cannot be verified. Require explicit user consent for degraded mode.

defences.name: Runtime Isolation Verification
defences.description: Continuously verify Docker daemon availability and container isolation (namespace + cgroup checks) before each execution

cwe_mapping: CWE-636 (Not Failing Securely / Fail Open), CWE-94 (Code Injection), CWE-749 (Exposed Dangerous Method)

poc.author: Yarden Porat / Cyata (original reporter)
poc.description: Stop Docker daemon → CrewAI agent with CodeInterpreter silently falls back to SandboxPython → ctypes escape → host RCE

environment.notes: CERT/CC VU#221883 — vendor acknowledged, no complete patch as of April 2026

poc.notes: CVE-2026-2275 (CVSS 9.7) and CVE-2026-2287 (CVSS 9.8). NO COMPLETE PATCH AVAILABLE.

events.notes: Reported to CERT/CC for coordinated disclosure
VU#221883 published. Vendor acknowledged, no complete patch.
